risk_factors.description: Outbound wire transfer amount ($48,250.00) exceeds the $10,000 high-risk threshold
risk_factors.evidence: parameters.amount = 48250.00; threshold defined in policy pol-fin-001 rule R-001 = 10000

risk_factors.description: Action initiated autonomously by an AI agent without prior human instruction
risk_factors.evidence: requested_by.entity_type = ai_agent; no matching human-initiated task found in active workflow queue

risk_factors.description: Target action is irreversible once wire settlement completes
risk_factors.evidence: action_type = finance.send_payment; payment_method = wire_transfer; reversibility classified as irreversible

risk_factors.description: Transaction requires recordkeeping under internal financial controls policy
risk_factors.evidence: Amount exceeds $5,000 reporting threshold defined in pol-fin-002

policy_flags.message: Wire transfers exceeding $10,000 require explicit human authorization before execution
All financial actions initiated by AI agents require human approval regardless of amount
Transaction exceeds $5,000 internal reporting threshold; logged for compliance records

notes: Two independent policies require human authorization for this request: the financial threshold policy (pol-fin-001) and the AI-initiated action review policy (pol-ai-003). Combined risk score of 82 reflects the high financial impact, autonomous AI origin, and irreversible nature of the wire transfer. Recommend single-approver authorization with 5-minute time-bound window.
